description: Someone records a meeting's decision where the group can return to it. 'sirami' creates or keeps a durable account of an event, observation, or decision. 'thekiro' (write) makes written language; a record may use writing or another medium, and durability alone gives it no authority.
articulatory_notes: Start 'si' by grooving unvoiced airflow between the raised tongue blade and alveolar ridge, then lift the tongue into high-front 'i'. For stressed 'ra', briefly tap or trill against the ridge before opening fully into central 'a'. End 'mi' by closing the lips while nasal voice continues into high-front unrounded 'i'.
sound_symbolism: A fine trace opens in 'si', broadens through stressed 'ra', and comes to rest behind the closed lips of 'mi'. The shape suits an account meant to remain.
usage_notes: The event, decision, observation, or other matter recorded precedes the verb. A nearby account can supply source, time, version, and access when those details matter. 'nelore' (preserve) protects an existing thing from loss; 'sirami' concerns the account itself.

pillars.solarpunk-values: A maintenance note or ecological observation can outlast the person who first noticed the condition. The record remains useful because its source and access stay recoverable.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: A durable account can keep testimony available after a meeting ends. Written or official form still does not erase competing accounts.

semantic_domains.communication: an account preserved in a form that others can receive later
semantic_domains.community: a shared or institutional account of events, observations, or decisions
